strInstructions: Rinse and cook the toor dal until soft. In a separate pan, heat oil or ghee and sauté cumin, mustard seeds, onions, ginger, garlic, and green chili. Add tomatoes and cook until softened. Stir in turmeric, red chili powder, and garam masala. Add the cooked dal and simmer until creamy and well combined. Garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

Score the chicken pieces and marinate them with lemon juice, yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, cumin, coriander, and salt. Rest for several hours or overnight. Roast, grill, or bake until cooked through and lightly charred. Brush with butter or oil before serving.

Sauté onions until soft, then add ginger-garlic paste and cook briefly. Add tomatoes and spices, and cook until the masala thickens. Stir in green peas and a little water and simmer until tender. Add paneer cubes and cook gently for a few minutes. Finish with garam masala and cilantro before serving.

Cook soaked kidney beans until tender. In a pan, sauté onions until golden, then add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Cook with turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and garam masala until the masala thickens. Add the kidney beans and simmer until the curry becomes rich and flavorful. Serve hot with rice.

Heat oil and sauté cumin and onions until soft. Add ginger, garlic, tomatoes, turmeric, chili powder, and coriander powder, then cook into a masala. Add potatoes and cauliflower and cook until coated. Cover and cook on low heat until tender, stirring occasionally. Finish with garam masala and cilantro.

Marinate the chicken with yogurt, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, and spices. Roast or pan-cook the chicken until lightly charred. Prepare a sauce by cooking tomato puree with butter, cream, chili powder, garam masala, and salt. Add the cooked chicken to the sauce and simmer until rich and well coated. Serve with naan or rice.

Cook the chana dal until tender but not mushy. In a pan, heat oil or ghee and sauté cumin, onions, ginger, garlic, and chili. Add tomatoes and spices and cook into a thick masala. Stir in the cooked dal and simmer until the flavors combine. Garnish with cilantro and serve hot.

Sauté onions until soft, then add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, and garam masala and cook the masala until thick. Stir in cooked chickpeas and simmer with a little water. Add paneer cubes near the end and cook gently for a few minutes. Garnish with cilantro and serve hot.

Brown onions and blend them into a smooth paste. Prepare a chili paste and kokum extract separately. Heat ghee, sauté whole spices, and cook the onion paste and chili paste until aromatic. Add garlic paste, chicken, turmeric, black pepper, salt, and kokum extract, then simmer until the chicken is cooked through. Finish with saffron and serve hot.

Wash and dry the okra completely, then slice it. Heat oil in a pan and sauté the okra until the slime reduces and the pieces are partly cooked. Remove and set aside. In the same pan, sauté cumin seeds, onions, ginger-garlic paste, and tomatoes until the masala thickens. Add turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, garam masala, and salt. Return the okra to the pan and cook until tender and coated with the masala. Serve hot with roti or rice.

Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, cumin, coriander powder, oil, and salt. Grill, bake, or pan-fry the chicken until lightly charred. For the masala, sauté onions in oil or butter until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste, tomatoes, and spices, then cook until thick. Add water, cream, and the cooked chicken, then simmer until the sauce coats the chicken. Finish with kasuri methi and coriander.

Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, and salt. Fry onions until golden, then blend some of them with yogurt, nuts, and spices to make a smooth paste. Heat oil or ghee and sauté whole spices. Add the marinated chicken and cook until sealed. Add the onion-nut paste and simmer until the chicken is tender and the sauce becomes creamy. Finish with coriander leaves and serve with rice, roti, or naan.

Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin, fennel, peppercorns, dried chilies, cinnamon, cloves, cardamom, and coconut until aromatic. Grind them into a paste or powder. Heat oil and sauté curry leaves, onions, ginger-garlic paste, and tomatoes until soft. Add chicken, turmeric, salt, and the Chettinad spice paste. Cook until the chicken releases moisture, then add water as needed and simmer until tender. Reduce the gravy until thick and spicy.

Marinate chicken pieces with ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, pepper, curry leaves, lemon juice, yogurt, egg, corn flour, rice flour, and salt. Rest briefly so the coating sticks. Deep-fry the chicken until crisp and cooked through. In a small pan, sauté garlic, green chilies, and curry leaves, then toss the fried chicken with the tempering. Serve hot with lemon wedges and onion slices.

Cut chicken into small pieces and marinate with ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, lemon juice, curry leaves, and salt. Add gram flour, rice flour, and a little water or egg only as needed to coat the chicken. Rest briefly. Heat oil and deep-fry the chicken pieces in batches until crisp, golden, and cooked through. Serve hot with chutney or lemon wedges.

Marinate fish pieces with turmeric, chili powder, and salt. Heat oil and sauté mustard seeds or cumin, curry leaves, onions, ginger-garlic paste, and green chilies. Add tomatoes and spices, then cook until soft. Add tamarind water or coconut milk depending on the variation, then simmer until the gravy is flavorful. Add the fish pieces gently and cook until just done. Rest briefly before serving with rice.

Clean prawns and season lightly with turmeric and salt. Heat oil and sauté onions until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste, tomatoes, curry leaves, and spices, then cook until the masala thickens. Add coconut milk or water and simmer briefly. Add prawns and cook only until they turn pink and tender. Do not overcook. Finish with coriander leaves and serve with rice.

Cook onions, tomatoes, cashews, ginger-garlic paste, and spices until soft and aromatic. Blend into a smooth sauce. Heat butter in a pan, add the sauce, and simmer until glossy. Add chili powder, garam masala, sugar, salt, and water as needed. Add paneer cubes and cook gently so they stay soft. Finish with cream and kasuri methi. Serve with naan, roti, or rice.

Rinse spinach well and blanch briefly or cook until wilted. Blend into a smooth puree with green chili if desired. Heat oil or ghee and sauté cumin seeds, onions, ginger, garlic, and tomatoes until soft. Add spices and cook until aromatic. Add the spinach puree and simmer briefly. Add paneer cubes and cook gently until warmed through. Finish with cream or butter if desired and serve with roti, naan, or rice.

Dry roast coriander seeds and dried red chilies, then crush into a coarse kadai masala. Heat oil or ghee and sauté onions, ginger-garlic paste, and tomatoes until thick. Add bell peppers and cook until slightly tender but still crisp. Add paneer cubes, kadai masala, garam masala, kasuri methi, and salt. Toss gently so the paneer is coated in the spicy tomato masala. Garnish with coriander leaves.

Soak chickpeas overnight and cook until tender, or use cooked chickpeas. Heat oil and sauté cumin, onions, ginger-garlic paste, and green chilies. Add tomatoes and cook until the masala thickens. Add chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, garam masala, and chana masala powder. Add chickpeas and some cooking liquid, then simmer until the chickpeas absorb the spices and the gravy thickens. Finish with coriander leaves and lemon juice.

Soak whole black gram and kidney beans overnight. Pressure cook until very soft. In a pot, sauté ginger-garlic paste in butter, then add tomato puree and spices. Cook until the tomato base thickens and the butter separates. Add the cooked lentils and beans with their liquid, then simmer slowly until creamy. Mash some lentils while simmering. Finish with cream, butter, and kasuri methi. Serve with naan or rice.

Rinse red lentils until the water runs clear. Cook lentils with water, turmeric, and salt until soft. In a small pan, heat ghee or oil and temper cumin seeds, garlic, dried red chili, and green chili. Add onions and tomatoes if using, then cook until soft. Pour the tempering into the cooked lentils and simmer briefly. Finish with coriander leaves and lemon juice.

Cook toor dal with turmeric until soft and mash it. Cook mixed vegetables with water, tamarind, salt, and sambar powder until tender. Add the cooked dal and simmer until the flavors combine. Prepare a tempering with oil, mustard seeds, dried red chili, curry leaves, asafoetida, and onions if using. Pour the tempering into the sambar and simmer briefly. Serve with rice, idli, or dosa.

Rinse rice, urad dal, and fenugreek seeds, then soak for 4 to 5 hours. Grind the soaked ingredients with water into a smooth batter. Mix with salt and ferment overnight until light and bubbly. Heat a dosa tawa or flat skillet. Pour a ladle of batter and spread thinly in circles. Drizzle oil around the edges and cook until crisp and golden. Fold and serve with chutney and sambar.

Prepare fermented dosa batter from rice and urad dal. For the filling, boil and mash potatoes. Heat oil and temper mustard seeds, cumin, curry leaves, green chilies, and ginger. Add onions and cook until soft. Add turmeric and mashed potatoes, then season with salt and coriander leaves. Spread dosa batter thinly on a hot tawa, cook until crisp, place potato masala in the center, fold, and serve with chutney and sambar.

Rinse rice and urad dal separately, then soak for several hours. Grind urad dal into a fluffy batter and rice into a slightly coarse batter. Mix together with salt and ferment overnight until risen. Grease idli molds and pour in the batter. Steam until the idli are soft and cooked through. Rest briefly before removing from molds. Serve with sambar and coconut chutney.

Dry roast semolina until aromatic, then set aside. Heat oil or ghee and temper mustard seeds, cumin, urad dal, chana dal, curry leaves, green chilies, ginger, and cashews if using. Add onions and cook until soft. Pour in water and season with salt. When the water boils, slowly add roasted semolina while stirring continuously to prevent lumps. Cover and cook until fluffy. Finish with coriander leaves and lemon juice.

Rinse flattened rice briefly until softened, then drain. Heat oil and temper mustard seeds, cumin, curry leaves, green chilies, and peanuts. Add onions and cook until soft. Add turmeric and potatoes if using, then cook until tender. Add the softened poha, salt, and sugar, then mix gently. Cover briefly to steam. Finish with coriander leaves, lemon juice, and sev if desired.

Boil and mash potatoes. Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, green chilies, ginger, garlic, turmeric, and asafoetida in oil. Mix with the mashed potatoes and shape into balls. Make a batter with gram flour, turmeric, chili powder, salt, and water. Dip the potato balls in the batter and deep-fry until golden. Place each vada inside a pav bun with green chutney, tamarind chutney, and dry garlic chutney.

Boil potatoes, cauliflower, carrots, peas, and other vegetables until soft. Mash well. Heat butter in a pan and sauté onions, capsicum, ginger-garlic paste, and tomatoes. Add pav bhaji masala, chili powder, and salt. Add the mashed vegetables and water as needed, then mash and simmer until thick and buttery. Toast pav buns with butter. Serve the bhaji with buttered pav, chopped onions, coriander, and lemon wedges.

Make a stiff dough with flour, carom seeds, salt, oil, and water. Rest the dough. For the filling, boil and mash potatoes. Temper cumin, ginger, green chili, peas, and spices, then mix with potatoes and coriander leaves. Divide the dough, roll into ovals, cut in half, and shape into cones. Fill with potato mixture and seal. Deep-fry on low to medium heat until crisp and golden. Serve with chutney.

Make a soft dough with whole wheat flour, salt, water, and a little oil. Rest the dough. For the filling, mash boiled potatoes with green chilies, coriander leaves, garam masala, cumin powder, amchur, chili powder, and salt. Roll a dough ball, place filling inside, seal, and roll gently into a flatbread. Cook on a hot tawa with ghee or oil until golden spots appear on both sides. Serve with yogurt, pickle, or butter.

Make a soft dough with flour, yogurt, yeast or baking powder, sugar, salt, oil, and warm water or milk. Rest until soft and slightly risen. Divide into balls and roll into flatbreads. Top with minced garlic and coriander leaves. Cook on a hot tawa or skillet until bubbles appear, then flip or roast directly over flame until charred in spots. Brush with butter and serve hot.

Rinse basmati rice until the water runs clear, then soak briefly. Heat ghee in a pot and fry cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, and cardamom until fragrant. Add drained rice and sauté gently. Pour in water and salt, then cook covered until the rice is fluffy and the grains are separate. Rest for a few minutes before fluffing with a fork.

Rinse and soak basmati rice, then cook until partially done if making dum biryani. Prepare vegetables such as carrots, beans, potatoes, cauliflower, and peas. Heat oil or ghee and sauté whole spices, onions, ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, tomatoes, mint, coriander leaves, and biryani spices. Add the vegetables and cook until partly tender. Layer the cooked rice and vegetable masala, then cook covered on low heat until the rice is fully done and aromatic. Rest before fluffing gently. Serve with raita.

Boil and mash potatoes, then mix with grated paneer, corn flour, salt, garam masala, and chopped nuts or raisins if using. Shape into balls and deep-fry until golden. For the gravy, cook onions, tomatoes, cashews, ginger-garlic paste, and spices until soft, then blend into a smooth sauce. Simmer the sauce with butter, cream, kasuri methi, and water as needed. Add the fried kofta just before serving so they stay soft but do not break.

Prepare mixed vegetables such as carrots, potatoes, beans, peas, cauliflower, and capsicum. Cook or steam them until just tender. Make a paste with cashews and other nuts if using. Heat ghee or oil and sauté whole spices, onions, ginger-garlic paste, and tomatoes. Add spices and the nut paste, then simmer into a creamy sauce. Add the vegetables, paneer, cream, and dried fruits. Cook briefly until everything is coated and rich. Serve with naan or rice.

Clean and chop fresh fenugreek leaves. Peel and cube potatoes. Heat oil and sauté cumin seeds, garlic, green chilies, and potatoes. Cook the potatoes until partly tender. Add turmeric, chili powder, and salt, then add the chopped methi leaves. Cook uncovered until the leaves wilt and the potatoes are fully cooked. Finish with garam masala or amchur if desired. Serve with roti or dal rice.

Boil potatoes until tender, then peel and cube them. Heat oil or ghee and add cumin seeds until they crackle. Add green chilies and ginger if using. Add the potatoes, turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, amchur, and salt. Toss gently until the potatoes are coated with spices and lightly crisp on the edges. Finish with coriander leaves and lemon juice. Serve with roti, poori, or dal rice.

Prepare chenna from milk and knead it until smooth. Shape into small flattened discs. Boil sugar and water to make syrup, then cook the chenna discs until they expand and become spongy. In another pot, simmer milk until slightly reduced. Add sugar, cardamom, saffron, and nuts. Gently squeeze the syrup from the cooked chenna discs and place them into the thickened flavored milk. Chill well before serving.
strMeasure1: 1 liter for chenna plus 1 liter for rabri

strInstructions: Prepare fresh chenna by curdling milk with lemon juice or vinegar, then drain well. Knead the chenna until smooth and soft. Mix with powdered sugar and cardamom. Cook the mixture gently on low heat only until it comes together and loses excess moisture. Do not overcook or it will become grainy. Cool slightly, shape into small sweets, and garnish with pistachios or saffron.

Make sugar syrup by boiling sugar, water, cardamom, and rose water until slightly sticky. For the dough, combine milk powder, flour, baking soda, ghee, and milk to form a soft dough. Rest briefly, then shape into smooth small balls without cracks. Fry the balls on low heat until evenly golden brown. Add the warm fried balls to warm syrup and let them soak until soft and juicy. Serve warm or at room temperature.

Rinse rice and soak briefly. Bring milk to a boil in a heavy pot. Add the drained rice and simmer on low heat, stirring often, until the rice becomes soft and the milk thickens. Add sugar and continue cooking until dissolved. Stir in cardamom powder, nuts, and raisins. Cook until creamy, then serve warm or chilled.

Rinse and soak basmati rice, then cook until partially done. Marinate paneer with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric, biryani masala, garam masala, mint, coriander leaves, and salt. Fry onions until golden. Layer the marinated paneer, fried onions, herbs, and rice in a heavy pot. Drizzle with saffron milk or ghee if using. Cover tightly and cook on low heat until the rice is fully done and the paneer absorbs the spices. Rest before serving with raita.

Rinse and soak basmati rice. Heat ghee or oil in a pot and sauté whole spices, onions, green chilies, ginger-garlic paste, mint, and coriander leaves until fragrant. Add tomatoes or yogurt if using, then add biryani masala and salt. Add drained rice and water. Cook covered until the rice is fluffy and the grains are separate. Rest for a few minutes before gently fluffing. Serve with curry, raita, or shorba.

Soak or boil soya chunks until soft, then rinse and squeeze out excess water. Marinate with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric, biryani masala, garam masala, mint, coriander leaves, and salt. Rinse and soak basmati rice. Sauté whole spices and onions in oil or ghee, then add the marinated soya chunks and cook until the masala thickens. Add rice and water, then cook covered until the rice is done. Rest before fluffing and serve with raita.

Heat ghee and fry cashews and raisins until golden, then set aside. Roast semolina in the same ghee until aromatic. Boil water with sugar and saffron or food color if using. Slowly add the roasted semolina while stirring continuously to prevent lumps. Cook until thick and glossy. Add cardamom powder, fried nuts, and raisins. Serve warm.

Add chilled yogurt, sugar, cardamom powder, and a little milk or water to a blender. Blend until smooth and frothy. Adjust sweetness and thickness as needed. Pour into glasses and garnish with chopped nuts, saffron, or rose water if desired. Serve chilled.

Add ripe mango pieces or mango pulp, chilled yogurt, sugar or honey, cardamom powder, and milk to a blender. Blend until smooth and creamy. Adjust the thickness with more milk if needed. Pour into glasses and garnish with chopped pistachios, saffron, or mint. Serve chilled.
